title: androsace elongata subsp. acuta
intro: Androsace elongata subsp. acuta, also known as the alpine rose or mountain rose, is a subspecies of the flowering plant Androsace elongata, which is native to the mountainous regions of Europe. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial activities. The plant is believed to have health benefits such as reducing inflammation, improving respiratory issues, and aiding in the treatment of various gastrointestinal problems. Androsace elongata subsp. acuta can be used in herbal preparations, including infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, which can be consumed as a tea or used as a topical application.
